name: Sketchup
summary: The Sketchup module contains a number of important utility methods for use in your Ruby scripts. Many of the classes in the API are implemented beneath this module. You can think of the Sketchup module as the “root” of the application tree. Most ruby calls start from the currently active model, and this is accessed via the Sketchup.active_model method.

methods.name: active_model
methods.summary: The active_model method returns the currently active SketchUp model.
methods.remarks: The active_model method returns the currently active SketchUp model. On the PC, this is the only model that one can have access to via the API, but Macintosh versions of SketchUp can have multiple models open at once, in which case the method will return the model that the user currently has focused.

returns.doc: active model object if successful, false if unsuccessful

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.

params.name: observer
params.doc: An observer.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: app_name
methods.summary: The app_name method is used to retrieve the current application name.
methods.remarks: The app_name method is used to retrieve the current application name.

returns.doc: the name of the application, either “SketchUp Pro” or “SketchUp”. Note: For versions earlier than SketchUp8 M4 (Mac 8.0.15157 and Windows 8.0.15158) this function will return “Google SketchUp Pro” or “Google SketchUp”.

methods.name: break_edges=
methods.summary: The break_edges= method can be used to disable or enable the break edges feature.
methods.remarks: The break_edges= method can be used to disable or enable the break edges feature. Break edges is the SketchUp 7 feature that automatically splits edges that the user draws which cross over one another. This feature is always on by default and cannot be disabled by the user via the user interface, but you can call this method to disable it. Be cautious in doing so, however, as the resulting model could then be altered when the user later draws lines into it with the break edges feature reactivated.

params.name: enabled
params.doc: If true, break edges will be turned on. If false, it will be deactivated.

returns.doc: true if break edges was turned on.

methods.name: break_edges?
methods.summary: The break_edges? method indicates whether the break edges feature is currently turned on.
methods.remarks: The break_edges? method indicates whether the break edges feature is currently turned on. Break edges is the SketchUp 7 feature that automatically splits edges that the user draws which cross over one another. This feature is always on by default and cannot be disabled by the user via the user interface.

methods.name: create_texture_writer
methods.summary: The create_texture_writer method is used to create a TextureWriter object.
methods.remarks: The create_texture_writer method is used to create a TextureWriter object.

returns.doc: a texturewriter object if successful.

methods.name: debug_mode=
methods.summary: Note: Changing this value within your extension can cause problems for other extension developers who rely on the debug information for their own work.
methods.remarks: Note: Changing this value within your extension can cause problems for other extension developers who rely on the debug information for their own work. Only use this locally; never change this value in an extension you publish. The debug_mode= method lets you controls whether SketchUp will output warnings to the console when it detects incorrect usage of the API. The setting takes effect right away, no need to restart SketchUp.

params.name: enabled
params.doc: If true, SketchUp will produce debug warnings.

methods.name: debug_mode?
methods.summary: The debug_mode? controls whether SketchUp will output warnings to the console when it detects incorrect usage of the API.
methods.remarks: The debug_mode? controls whether SketchUp will output warnings to the console when it detects incorrect usage of the API.

methods.name: display_name_from_action
methods.summary: Note: This method has been non-functional on Mac since SketchUp 8.
methods.remarks: Note: This method has been non-functional on Mac since SketchUp 8. The display_name_from_action method is used to gets a user-friendly name from an action string. See Sketchup.send_action for a list of valid action strings.

params.name: action_name
params.doc: An action string.

returns.doc: a friendly name.

methods.name: extensions
methods.summary: Returns the ExtensionsManager where you can find all registered SketchupExtension objects.
methods.remarks: Returns the ExtensionsManager where you can find all registered SketchupExtension objects. #Sketchup.extensionextension.loaded?“

returns.doc: an ExtensionsManager object.

methods.name: file_new
methods.summary: The file_new method is used to create a new file.
methods.remarks: The file_new method is used to create a new file.

returns.doc: The Sketchup module.

methods.name: find_support_file
methods.summary: The find_support_files method is used to retrieve the relative path and name of a file within the SketchUp installation directory.
methods.remarks: The find_support_files method is used to retrieve the relative path and name of a file within the SketchUp installation directory. Forward slashes must be used to delimit between directory names.

params.name: filename
params.doc: Name of the filename you want to find.

params.name: directory
params.doc: directory relative to the SketchUp installation directory.

returns.doc: the entire path if successful. If unsuccessful, the method returns false.

methods.name: find_support_files
methods.summary: The find_support_files method is used to retrieve the path and name of all matching files within the SketchUp installation directory.
methods.remarks: The find_support_files method is used to retrieve the path and name of all matching files within the SketchUp installation directory. Forward slashes must be used to delimit between directory names.

params.name: filename
params.doc: Extension of the files to be found.

params.name: directory
params.doc: directory relative to the SketchUp installation directory. Without this the result will be empty.

returns.doc: an array of files. If unsuccessful, the method returns false.

methods.name: fix_shadow_strings=
methods.summary: The fix_shadow_strings= method lets you control whether shadow rendering attempts to fix an artifact commonly referred to as “strings”.
methods.remarks: The fix_shadow_strings= method lets you control whether shadow rendering attempts to fix an artifact commonly referred to as “strings”. The fix is actually very model dependent and not controllable from the UI, so this method can be used to control it.

params.name: enabled
params.doc: If true, shadow strings fix will be turned on. If false, it will be deactivated.

returns.doc: true if shadow strings fix was turned on.

methods.name: fix_shadow_strings?
methods.summary: The fix_shadow_strings? method indicates whether the a fix for a shadow rendering artifact commonly referred to as “strings” is enabled.
methods.remarks: The fix_shadow_strings? method indicates whether the a fix for a shadow rendering artifact commonly referred to as “strings” is enabled. The fix is actually very model dependent and not controllable from the UI, so this method can be used to test it.

methods.name: focus
methods.summary: The focus method is used to focus the active model window.
methods.remarks: The focus method is used to focus the active model window.

methods.name: format_angle
methods.summary: The format_angle method takes a number as an angle in radians and formats it into degrees.
methods.remarks: The format_angle method takes a number as an angle in radians and formats it into degrees. For example, format_angle(Math::PI) will return 180.0.

params.name: number
params.doc: A number to be formatted.

returns.doc: an angle in degrees if successful, false if unsuccessful

methods.name: format_area
methods.summary: The format_area method formats a number as an area using the current units settings.
methods.remarks: The format_area method formats a number as an area using the current units settings. The number must be in square inches.

params.name: number
params.doc: A number to be formatted.

methods.name: format_degrees
methods.summary: The format_degrees method formats a number as an angle given in degrees.
methods.remarks: The format_degrees method formats a number as an angle given in degrees. For example, 10 becomes 10.0. This is the equivalent to a to_f call.

params.name: number
params.doc: A number to be formatted.

returns.doc: degrees if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: format_length
methods.summary: The format_length method formats a number as a length using the current units settings.
methods.remarks: The format_length method formats a number as a length using the current units settings. The default unit setting is inches. For example, 10 becomes 10“.

params.name: number
params.doc: A number to be formatted.

methods.name: format_volume
methods.summary: The format_volume method formats a number as a volume using the current units settings.
methods.remarks: The format_volume method formats a number as a volume using the current units settings. The number must be in cubic inches.

params.name: number
params.doc: A number to be formatted.

methods.name: get_datfile_info
methods.summary: The get_datfile_info method is used to retrieve the value for the given key from Sketchup.
methods.remarks: The get_datfile_info method is used to retrieve the value for the given key from Sketchup.dat. If the key is not found, default_value is returned.

params.name: key
params.doc: The key whose value you want to retrieve.

params.name: default_value
params.doc: The default value you want returned if key is not available.

returns.doc: a string value if successful.

methods.name: get_i18n_datfile_info
methods.summary: The get_i18n_datfile_info method is used to retrieve the value for the given key from the internationalization file that SketchUp uses to work in multiple languages.
methods.remarks: The get_i18n_datfile_info method is used to retrieve the value for the given key from the internationalization file that SketchUp uses to work in multiple languages. If the key is not found, default_value is returned.

params.name: key
params.doc: The key whose value you want to retrieve.

params.name: default_value
params.doc: The default value you want returned if key is not available.

methods.name: get_locale
methods.summary: The os_language method returns the language code for the language SketchUp is running in.
methods.remarks: The os_language method returns the language code for the language SketchUp is running in. This is an alias for the get_locale method. Examples of return values are: en-US, fr, it, de, es, ja, ko, zh-CN, zh-TW, pt-BR, nl, ru and sv. For an up to date list os supported languages, see the SketchUp download page.

returns.doc: a code representing the language SketchUp is displaying.

methods.name: get_resource_path
methods.summary: The get_resource_path is used to retrieve the directory where “resource” files are stored by SketchUp.
methods.remarks: The get_resource_path is used to retrieve the directory where “resource” files are stored by SketchUp. Resource files include things like language localization files.

params.name: filename
params.doc: The filename of a resource file in the resource directory hierarchy.

returns.doc: the directory path to the resources folder.

methods.name: get_shortcuts
methods.summary: The get_shortcuts method retrieves an array of all keyboard shortcuts currently registered with SketchUp.
methods.remarks: The get_shortcuts method retrieves an array of all keyboard shortcuts currently registered with SketchUp. Each shortcut is returned as a string with the shortcut and the command separated by a tab, similar to “Ctrl+A\tEdit/Select All”

returns.doc: an array of shortcut strings.

methods.name: install_from_archive
methods.summary: Installs the contents of a ZIP archive file into SketchUp's Plugins folder.
methods.remarks: Installs the contents of a ZIP archive file into SketchUp's Plugins folder. If the ZIP contains subfolders, these will be preserved. This allows for a Ruby API plugin or Extension developer to distribute their plugin as a single file regardless of how many asset files must be included. The user will be shown a warning message that they must agree to before the install proceeds. If they do not agree, an Interrupt error will be raised. If the user does agree but there is a problem with the unzip process, an Exception will be raised. You can capture these states via a begin/rescue. See the example below. If the install is successful, any Ruby files that have been added to the Plugins folder will immediately be executed, saving the user a restart. To create an archive file, use your favorite tool (7zip, Winzip, etc.) to zip up any files and folders in your plugins directory. If the archive contains a SketchupExtension that you would like users to be able to install from the Preferences > Extensions panel, rename your file to have a .rbz file extension.

params.name: filepath
params.doc: The path to the RBZ or ZIP file to install.

params.name: show_warning
params.doc: Whether to warn the user not to install untrusted extensions. In certain cases the warning can be confusing and redundant, e.g. when automatically updating a trusted extension. When the user has selected the archive themselves, it is best to warn about the possible risks.

throws.when: If the archive cannot be installed.
If the user cancel the installation.
If the archive cannot be found.

methods.name: is_64bit?
methods.summary: This methods indicates whether the host SketchUp application is 64bit.
methods.remarks: This methods indicates whether the host SketchUp application is 64bit. Useful for extensions that ship with binaries and need to determine which versions to load.

methods.name: is_online
methods.summary: The is_online method is used to verify a connection to the Internet.
methods.remarks: The is_online method is used to verify a connection to the Internet. This method can take some time to execute, so be careful not to call it more often than you need.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: is_pro?
methods.summary: Note: In SketchUp Make this method will return true during the Pro trial period and revert to false when the trial period is over.
methods.remarks: Note: In SketchUp Make this method will return true during the Pro trial period and revert to false when the trial period is over. Returns a boolean flag indicating whether the application is SketchUp Pro.

methods.name: is_valid_filename?
methods.summary: The is_valid_filename? method is used to determine whether a filename contains illegal characters.
methods.remarks: The is_valid_filename? method is used to determine whether a filename contains illegal characters.

params.name: filename
params.doc: A filename string.

methods.name: load
methods.summary: The load method is used to load Ruby files.
methods.remarks: The load method is used to load Ruby files. Unlike Ruby's own load method it also supports SketchUp's encrypted .rbe files. You do not need to include the file extension in the path. This method will look for .rb first (unencrypted) and then .rbe (encrypted) and finally .rbs (the deprecated scrambled format) files. See the “Distributing your Plugin” article for details.

params.name: path
params.doc: The path, including the filename, to the file you want to require.

returns.doc: True if the file is included. False if the file is not included.

methods.name: open_file
methods.signature: open_file(filename) => Boolean | open_file(filename, with_status: true) => Integer, false | open_file(filename, with_status: true, show_version_warning_dialog: true) => Integer, false
methods.summary: The open_file method is used to open a SketchUp model.
methods.remarks: The open_file method is used to open a SketchUp model.

params.name: filename
params.doc: The model file to open.

returns.doc: true if opening the file succeeded, false otherwise.

methods.name: os_language
methods.summary: The os_language method returns the language code for the language SketchUp is running in.
methods.remarks: The os_language method returns the language code for the language SketchUp is running in. This is an alias for the get_locale method. Examples of return values are: en-US, fr, it, de, es, ja, ko, zh-CN, zh-TW, pt-BR, nl, ru and sv. For an up to date list os supported languages, see the SketchUp download page.

returns.doc: a code representing the language SketchUp is displaying.

methods.name: parse_length
methods.summary: The parse_length method parses a string as a length.
methods.remarks: The parse_length method parses a string as a length. For example, “200” becomes 200.0.

params.name: string
params.doc: The string to be parsed as a number.

returns.doc: the numerical representation of the string if successful, or nil if unsuccessful.

methods.name: platform
methods.summary: This methods returns a symbol indicating the current platform.
methods.remarks: This methods returns a symbol indicating the current platform. It should be used over RUBY_PLATFORM as this returns a different value for Windows since SketchUp 2014. Older SketchUp versions still need to check RUBY_PLATFORM.include?('mswin') or RUBY_PLATFORM.include?('darwin'). Possible return values: :platform_win :platform_osx

returns.doc: Current OS platform.

methods.name: plugins_disabled=
methods.summary: The plugins_disabled= method lets you control whether SketchUp will load Ruby scripts from the plugins directory at startup time.
methods.remarks: The plugins_disabled= method lets you control whether SketchUp will load Ruby scripts from the plugins directory at startup time. This is primarily a trouble-shooting method. If you are having strange behavior in SketchUp that you suspect is from a bad script, you can type Sketchup.plugins_disabled=true into the Ruby console and restart SketchUp to see if the problem is fixed.

params.name: enabled
params.doc: If true, the plugins directory will not load.

returns.doc: true if plugins were disabled.

methods.name: plugins_disabled?
methods.summary: The plugins_disabled? method indicates whether Ruby scripts in the plugins directory will be loaded at startup time.
methods.remarks: The plugins_disabled? method indicates whether Ruby scripts in the plugins directory will be loaded at startup time.

methods.name: quit
methods.summary: The quit method is used to terminate the application.
methods.remarks: The quit method is used to terminate the application. This will pop-up the usual model save prompts if there are unsaved models open. User can cancel the model save, in which case the application will not terminate.

returns.doc: self

methods.name: read_default
methods.summary: Note: Be aware that the method is not capable of handling Length objects.
methods.remarks: Note: Be aware that the method is not capable of handling Length objects. You can convert the value to a Float before writing and convert back to Length when reading the value. Don't store the value as a String as this rounds the value and formats it in a way that can't be read if the system setting for decimal separator changes. The #read_default method is used to retrieve the string associated with a value within the specified sub-section section of a .INI file or registry (within the Software > SketchUp > SketchUp [Version] section).

params.name: section
params.doc: A section in an .INI or registry.

params.name: variable
params.doc: A variable within the section.

params.name: default
params.doc: A default value if the value is not found.

returns.doc: if unsuccessful, the value of the default if successful.

methods.name: redo
methods.summary: The redo method is used redo the last transaction on the redo stack.
methods.remarks: The redo method is used redo the last transaction on the redo stack.

methods.name: register_extension
methods.summary: Note: It is recommended to set load_on_start to true unless you have a very good reason not to.
methods.remarks: Note: It is recommended to set load_on_start to true unless you have a very good reason not to. The #register_extension method is used to register an extension with SketchUp's Extension Manager.

params.name: extension
params.doc: A SketchupExtension object.

params.name: load_on_start
params.doc: Passing true into this will load the extension immediately and set it so that it will load automatically whenever SketchUp restarts.

returns.doc: true if extension registered properly

methods.name: register_importer
methods.summary: The register_importer method is used to register an importer with SketchUp.
methods.remarks: The register_importer method is used to register an importer with SketchUp.

params.name: importer
params.doc: An Importer object representing the importer.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.

params.name: observer
params.doc: An observer.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: require
methods.summary: The require method is used to load Ruby files once.
methods.remarks: The require method is used to load Ruby files once. Unlike Ruby's own require method it also supports SketchUp's encrypted .rbe files. You do not need to include the file extension in the path. This method will look for .rbe first (encrypted) and then .rbs (the deprecated scrambled format) and finally .rb (unencrypted) files. The loading order was changed in SketchUp 2016 when the new .rbe encryption was introduced. Prior to SketchUp 2016 the loading order was first .rb then .rbs.

params.name: path
params.doc: The path, including the filename, to the file you want to require.

returns.doc: True if the file is included. False if the file is not included.

methods.name: resize_viewport
methods.signature: resize_viewport(model, width, height) => Boolean | resize_viewport(model, width, height, logical_pixels: false) => Boolean
methods.summary: Note: In SketchUp 2024.
methods.remarks: Note: In SketchUp 2024.0 and later this method doesn't behave correctly in all cases on Windows. The passed values are internally converted to logical pixels, rounded and converted back to physical pixels. This means certain sizes, such as 1000 px at 150% scaling, cannot be accurately set. As a workaround in you can use sizes that are evenly divisible with common scale factors, if you depend on pixel perfect sizes. The resize_viewport method changes the pixel size of the viewport and SketchUp window. This can be useful for producing a consistent behavior in automatic testing, regardless of the display resolution.

params.name: width
params.doc: Width in physical pixels

params.name: height
params.doc: Height in physical pixels

returns.doc: true on success. false if the window couldn't reach the desired size, e.g. because it wouldn't fit the screen.

methods.name: save_thumbnail
methods.summary: The save_thumbnail method is used to generate a thumbnail for any SKP file - not necessarily the loaded model.
methods.remarks: The save_thumbnail method is used to generate a thumbnail for any SKP file - not necessarily the loaded model.

params.name: skp_filename
params.doc: The name of the SketchUp file whose model you want represented in the thumbnail.

params.name: img_filename
params.doc: The name of the file where the thumbnail will be saved.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: send_action
methods.summary: The send_action method sends a message to the message queue to perform some action asynchronously.
methods.remarks: The send_action method sends a message to the message queue to perform some action asynchronously. Valid actions are: showRubyPanel: viewBack: viewBottom: viewFront: viewIso: viewLeft: viewRight: viewTop: viewPerspective: viewShowAxes: viewShowHidden: viewZoomExtents: viewZoomToSelection: viewUndo: selectOrbitTool: selectPositionCameraTool: selectDollyTool: selectTurnTool: selectWalkTool: selectZoomTool: selectFieldOfViewTool: selectZoomWindowTool: pageAdd: pageDelete: pageUpdate: pageNext: pagePrevious: renderWireframe: renderHiddenLine: renderMonochrome: renderShaded: renderTextures: selectArcTool: selectArc3PointTool: selectArc3PointPieTool: selectAxisTool: selectCircleTool: selectEraseTool: selectFreehandTool: selectLineTool: selectMeasureTool: selectMoveTool: selectOffsetTool: selectPaintTool: selectPolygonTool: selectProtractorTool: selectPushPullTool: selectRectangleTool: selectRectangle3PointTool: selectRotateTool: selectScaleTool: selectSectionPlaneTool: selectTextTool: selectDimensionTool: selectExtrudeTool: selectSelectionTool: editUndo: editRedo: editHide: editUnhide: fixNonPlanarFaces: Added in SketchUp 8.0+: addBuilding: getPhotoTexture: selectImageIglooTool: selectNorthTool: Added in SketchUp 2013+: showExtensionStore: Removed in SketchUp 2013+: addBuilding: On the PC only, you can also send these numeric values. params.name: action
params.doc: The action to be performed.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful

methods.name: send_to_layout
methods.summary: The send_to_layout method is used to open a file in LayOut.
methods.remarks: The send_to_layout method is used to open a file in LayOut.

params.name: file
params.doc: The path and filename to open, either .skp or .layout.

returns.doc: true if opening the file succeeded, false otherwise. If LayOut is not installed or the file is not present this function will return false.

methods.name: set_status_text
methods.signature: set_status_text => nil | set_status_text(status_text = '', position = SB_PROMPT) => nil
methods.summary: The set_status_text method is used to set the text appearing on the status bar within the drawing window.
methods.remarks: The set_status_text method is used to set the text appearing on the status bar within the drawing window. If no arguments are passed, the status bar content is cleared. Valid positions are: SB_PROMPT - the text will appear at the left-side of the status bar SB_VCB_LABEL - the text will appear in place of the VCB label SB_VCB_VALUE - the text will appear in the VCB

params.name: status
params.doc: text the status text that will appear.

params.name: position
params.doc: the position where the text will appear.

methods.name: status_text=
methods.summary: The status_text= method is used to set the text appearing on the status bar within the drawing window.
methods.remarks: The status_text= method is used to set the text appearing on the status bar within the drawing window. This is the same as calling set_status_text with a 2nd parameter of SB_PROMPT.

params.name: status_text
params.doc: The status text that will appear.

methods.name: temp_dir
methods.summary: The temp_dir method is used to retrieve the OS temporary directory for the current user.
methods.remarks: The temp_dir method is used to retrieve the OS temporary directory for the current user. You can use this directory to write temporary files that are not required to persist between SketchUp sessions.

returns.doc: a string containing the full temporary directory path

methods.name: template
methods.summary: The template method is used to get the file name of the current template.
methods.remarks: The template method is used to get the file name of the current template. Templates are the .skp files that are loaded when the user select File > New.

returns.doc: the current template

methods.name: template=
methods.summary: The template= method is used to set the file name of the current template.
methods.remarks: The template= method is used to set the file name of the current template. Templates are the .skp files that are loaded when the user select File > New.

params.name: filename
params.doc: The name of the template to set.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: template_dir
methods.summary: The template_dir is used to retrieve the directory where templates are stored by the SketchUp install.
methods.remarks: The template_dir is used to retrieve the directory where templates are stored by the SketchUp install. Templates are the .skp files that are loaded when the user select File > New.

returns.doc: containing the full template directory path

methods.name: undo
methods.summary: The undo method is used undo the last transaction on the undo stack.
methods.remarks: The undo method is used undo the last transaction on the undo stack.

methods.name: vcb_label=
methods.summary: The vcb_label= method is used to set the label that appears on the vcb, or the “value control box”, which is another word for the “measurements” text entry box that appears at the bottom on the SketchUp window.
methods.remarks: The vcb_label= method is used to set the label that appears on the vcb, or the “value control box”, which is another word for the “measurements” text entry box that appears at the bottom on the SketchUp window. This is the same as calling set_status_text with a 2nd parameter of SB_VCB_LABEL.

params.name: label_text
params.doc: The label text that will appear.

methods.name: vcb_value=
methods.summary: The vcb_value= method is used to set the value that appears on the vcb, or the “value control box”, which is another word for the “measurements” text entry box that appears at the bottom on the SketchUp window.
methods.remarks: The vcb_value= method is used to set the value that appears on the vcb, or the “value control box”, which is another word for the “measurements” text entry box that appears at the bottom on the SketchUp window. This is the same as calling set_status_text with a 2nd parameter of SB_VCB_VALUE.

params.name: value
params.doc: The text that will appear as the vcb's value.

methods.name: version
methods.summary: Gets the current version of sketchup in decimal form.
methods.remarks: Gets the current version of sketchup in decimal form.

returns.doc: the decimal form of the version

methods.name: version_number
methods.summary: Get the current version of sketchup as a whole number for comparisons.
methods.remarks: Get the current version of sketchup as a whole number for comparisons. The number returned has the major, minor, and build values packed into an integer value as follows: Major version = X Minor version = Y Build number = Z SketchUp 6.0 - SketchUp 2015 XXYYYZZZ SketchUp 2016+ XXYZZZZZZZ

returns.doc: the whole number form of the version

methods.name: write_default
methods.summary: Note: Be aware that the method is not capable of handling Length objects.
methods.remarks: Note: Be aware that the method is not capable of handling Length objects. You can convert the value to a Float before writing and convert back to Length when reading the value. Don't store the value as a String as this rounds the value and formats it in a way that can't be read if the system setting for decimal separator changes. The #write_default method is used to set the string associated with a variable within the specified sub-section of a .plist file on the Mac or the registry on Windows (within the Software > SketchUp > SketchUp [Version] section).

params.name: section
params.doc: A section in a .plist file (Mac) or the registry (Windows).

params.name: key
params.doc: A key within the section.

params.name: value
params.doc: The value to store.

returns.doc: True if successful, false if unsuccessful.
